reason: InMemoryConnector keeps jobs in a dict keyed by job id and reset() reinitializes that state; app.replace_connector() in app.py is a context manager that restores the previous connector in a finally block.

run_worker() is synchronous (asyncio.run), wait=False makes the worker drain available jobs then stop, and with InMemoryConnector all fetch/defer/finish paths are in-process; listen_notify only controls the listener side-task (source-derived).
Docs and code agree that delete_jobs supports never/successful/always and defaults to never when unset.
The builtin remove_old_jobs task takes max_hours and calls context.app.job_manager.delete_old_jobs(nb_hours=max_hours, ...).

Each App() registers builtin tasks, add_tasks_from() mutates shared Task names via add_namespace() (so repeated App construction can stack builtin: prefixes), issue #521 documents duplicate-prefix behavior, and schema.sql defines task_name as character varying(128).
Worker heartbeat default is 10s, get_stalled_jobs defaults seconds_since_heartbeat to 30s, and the docs recommend periodic recovery with get_stalled_jobs()+retry_job() protected by a queueing lock.

Most points are documented (plain SQL, schema --migrations-path, manual tracking, pre/deploy/post flow), but filename pattern is not universally {version}_{serial}_{pre|post}_... because many shipped legacy migrations omit pre/post.
Docs describe procrastinate_events as timestamped job-event rows, list deferred/started/deferred_for_retry/failed/succeeded among event types, and label the API as early-stage.

Logging is emitted from module/child loggers (e.g., logging.getLogger(__name__) in procrastinate.worker), and multiple records are logged without extra.action, so both absolutes in the claim overstate behavior.

Docker docs state docker stop sends SIGTERM then SIGKILL after a grace period, and ECS StopTask API documentation says it issues the equivalent of docker stop and defaults to SIGTERM (then SIGKILL after timeout).
ECS task definition docs define stopTimeout as pre-force-kill wait time, with default 30 seconds when unset and max 120 seconds on Fargate.

Procrastinate 3.9.0 depends on psycopg[pool] (PyPI), Psycopg raises ImportError 'no pq wrapper available' when neither system-libpq-backed nor binary wrappers load, and psycopg[binary] adds the binary wrapper; python:3.12-slim runtime deps omit libpq (source-derived runtime conclusion).

Dockerfile reference states shell form runs under /bin/sh -c and that shell-form ENTRYPOINT does not pass signals/PID1 semantics, so exec-form command invocation is the signal-safe pattern for worker processes.
CLI defines --shutdown-graceful-timeout exactly as a shutdown wait-before-abort setting, and worker shutdown code aborts remaining jobs with AbortReason.SHUTDOWN.
